description_long: This cathedral features two massive cylindrical towers with crenellated tops, flanking a central nave structure. The towers are adorned with tall, narrow arched windows and subtle vertical ribbing, giving them a fortress-like appearance. The central section is lower and wider, with a series of smaller arched windows and a prominent main entrance at the base. The facade is largely unornamented, with minimal decorative elements aside from the window tracery and battlement-style parapets. The structure sits on a green base, suggesting a landscaped setting. The overall design is imposing and symmetrical, emphasizing verticality and solidity.
description: A grand, symmetrical cathedral with two cylindrical towers, crenellated tops, and a central nave featuring arched windows and a prominent entryway.
description_view_0: Front view showing one cylindrical tower with arched windows and the central nave partially visible behind.
description_view_1: Side view displaying both cylindrical towers, the full width of the central nave, and the main entrance at the center.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the second cylindrical tower in the foreground and the nave behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing both towers and the central nave with the entry portal visible.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: The facade is minimally ornamented, with vertical ribbing, crenellations, and arched windows as the primary decorative features. There are no visible statues or elaborate reliefs.
attribute_entry_portal_design: The main entry portal is centrally located, featuring a pointed arch and a modest set of steps leading up to it. Decorative elements are limited, with the focus on the arch shape and flanking windows.
